name: Bill Graham Civic Auditorium

permits.description: strengthen structural sys to upgrade seismic resistance
install new single ply membrane roof over existing rof membrane throughout the low slope roof of the bill graham ci vic auditorium. approx 65000 sf of roof area to be reroofed.

3rd flr only - commencement of work not started under previously approved permit applications pa 201612285994 & 201707262960. t.i. to modify men's restroom into all gender restroom. modiy dressing rm into new all gender restroom. modify electrical and mech related renovation.

renovate (e) polk st banquet hall on 1st flr, new wall system supporting decorative perforated metal panels/acoustic materials, new acoustic t-bar ceiling/lighting, accessibility upgrades to (e)men's/women's restrooms. no work to (e) mechanical/plumbing systems.

replacement of (e) outside air, mixing and return dampers and filters of main arena fans. replacement of motors and bearings, installation of associated variable frequency drives and controls. emer lighting under sep permit.
upgrade of emergency lighting in the building. new light fixtures, controls

summary_en: Archived documents cover the auditorium as a city convention and events venue, including a 1994 draft transition plan for the Department of Convention Facilities on the "Bill Graham Civic Auditorium and Brooks Exhibit Hall sites" and a 1965 study of the economic performance of the Civic Auditorium-Brooks Hall complex, alongside 2000 Finance and Labor Committee minutes on permits to operate there. It falls under the City Administrator (ADM), whose FY2025 budget line totaled $288M. An Arts Commission contract, "Papa-Civic Auditorium Main," records about $96K paid to Decker Electric Company.

documents.title: Draft transition plan for the Department of Convention Facilities : Civic Auditorium and Brooks Exhibit Hall sites
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Dept. of Convention Facilities,San Francisco (Calif.). Bureau of Architecture
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1994 Department of Convention Facilities draft transition plan addressing ADA structural changes to the Bill Graham Civic Auditorium and Brooks Exhibit Hall, then under seismic renovation.

documents.title: The Economic performance of public facilities in Yerba Buena Center
documents.creator: San Francisco Redevelopment Agency (San Francisco, Calif.),Economics Research Associates
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1965 Yerba Buena Center facilities study reporting the Warriors' 1964-65 season in the refurbished San Francisco Civic Auditorium, its 1963 closure for remodeling, and the Civic Auditorium-Brooks Hall complex as a major events venue.
